studies.title: Pilot Randomized Controlled Study of the Impact of MedRhythms' MR-010 in Acute Stroke
studies.official_title: Pilot Randomized Controlled Study of the Impact of MedRhythms' MR-010 in Acute Stroke

interventions.name: MR-010
interventions.description: MR-010 includes a patient application and sensors with commercially available headphones and smartphones. Sensors are placed on the subject's shoes and measure gait metrics. The algorithm uses data from the sensors to inform changes made to the audio cues. These cues, which are embedded in time-shifted music, are delivered back to the users who aim to walk to the beat.

studies.brief_summary: The purpose of this clinical pilot study is to evaluate the effects of the MR-010 on tolerability, biomechanics and walking speed in the acute stroke care setting in addition to its impact on length of stay.

_verification.reason: The study is specifically about the MR-010 digital therapeutic product, as evidenced by the product name 'MR-010' appearing in the title, abstract, and detailed intervention description. The study evaluates the effects of MR-010 on gait deficits in acute stroke patients, aligning with the product's intended use.

_classification.reason: The study is described as a 'Pilot Randomized Controlled Study' with random allocation to intervention, fitting the definition of an RCT as it involves an interventional, experimental design with randomization to isolate the treatment effect.
